Creates simulated data for TDLNM

Source: R/sim.tdlnm.R
sim.tdlnm.Rd

Method for creating simulated data for TDLNM

Usage

sim.tdlnm(sim = "A", error.to.signal = 1)

Arguments

sim

character (A - D) specifying simulation scenario

error.to.signal

scalar value setting error: sigma^2/var(f)

Value

Simulated data and true parameters

Details

sim.tdlnm

Simulation scenarios:

  • Scenario A: Piecewise constant effect

  • Scenario B: Linear effect

  • Scenario C: Logistic effect, piecewise in time

  • Scenario D: Logistic effect, smooth in time

Examples

sim.tdlnm(sim = "A", error.to.signal = 1)
#> $dat
#>                  y           c1            c2           c3            c4

#> 
#> $dlnm.fun
#> function (exposure.data, cenval, sum = TRUE) 
#> {
#>     if (sum) {
#>         -rowSums(exposure.data[, 11:15] > 2)
#>     }
#>     else {
#>         dlnm <- t(sapply(1:nrow(exposure.data), function(i) {
#>             sapply(1:ncol(exposure.data), function(j) {
#>                 ifelse(j %in% 11:15, ifelse(exposure.data[i, 
#>                   j] > 2, -1, 0), 0)
#>             })
#>         }))
#>         colnames(dlnm) <- paste0("Time", 1:ncol(exposure.data))
#>         return(dlnm)
#>     }
#> }

#> <environment: 0x55c89742e0e8>
